mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments. It is ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development scenarios where a full Kubernetes installation is not feasible. K3s simplifies the installation process by bundling all necessary components into a single binary, making it easy to deploy and manage.

steps.title: Install K3s Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your system, which is required to run the K3s cluster.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command downloads and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
After installation, verify that K3s is running correctly by checking the status of the service.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command checks the status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig file to interact with the K3s cluster using kubectl.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to the K3s kubeconfig file.
> This ensures that the kubeconfig file has the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the K3s cluster nodes are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the K3s cluster.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Optionally, you can set up monitoring and autoscaling for your K3s cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k3s-io/k3s/master/manifests/metrics-server.yaml

```

> This installs the metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data in the cluster.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After uninstalling K3s, check for any orphaned resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes.

```bash

kubectl get services --all-namespaces

kubectl get pvc --all-namespaces

```

> Check for any remaining services.
> Check for any remaining persistent volume claims.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your cluster data. Then, perform the upgrade and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup-$(date +%F).yaml

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup all resources in the cluster.
> This command upgrades K3s to the latest version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the K3s logs with 'journalctl -u k3s' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** K3s may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Ensure K3s is installed and running with 'systemctl status k3s'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure KUBECONFIG is set to the correct path: 'export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check node resources with 'kubectl describe nodes' and adjust resource requests.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new CNI plugins, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older storage backends.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure that your storage classes are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible system with sufficient resources.
